title: docker - support for saving binaries outside container
author: majso

title: :sparkles: Make it possible to dynamically load node packages
body:
First POC to make it possible to dynamically load additional credentials/nodes at runtime from npm.

Info:
 - does not work yet with queue-mode
 - did not do proper testing yet
 - no UI implemented, only REST endpoint
 - only works for nodes that do not have additional dependencies

Test:
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:5678/rest/node \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{"url": "https://registry.npmjs.org/@digital-boss/n8n-nodes-zammad/-/n8n-nodes-zammad-1.1.2.tgz"}'
```
author: janober

title: Remove redundant tsconfig options
body:
These tsconfig options default to `true` when [`strict`](https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig#strict ) is enabled.

- [`strictNullChecks`](https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig#strictNullChecks)
- [`noImplicitAny`](https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig#noImplicitAny)

Note: /cli is does not have `strict` enabled. Most of the value of TS is under this flag - we should fix this soon.
author: ivov

title: Fixed execute commands after binary data release
body:
With the release of binary data management, the execute commands broke.

This PR fixes the binary data handler initialization so these commands can continue working.

title: 🐛 Fix Philips Hue API Connection
body:
This PR fixes the problem reported in the forum at https://community.n8n.io/t/cannot-link-with-philips-hue-the-requested-permission-could-not-be-verified/10024?u=mutedjam

Philips uses new OAuth2 URLs as per https://developers.meethue.com/develop/hue-api/remote-authentication-oauth/ which have been added to the credentials.

As per https://developers.meethue.com/develop/hue-api/remote-api-quick-start-guide/, the remote API endpoints have also been updated:

> From now on the Remote API calls are same as local API calls. Just the base URL is different: https://api.meethue.com/route/api/<whitelist_identifier>instead of http://<ip-address.of.the.bridge>/api/<whitelist_identifier>

This PR reflects this change as well and sets the base URL to `https://api.meethue.com/route`, with the endpoints used in the `PhilipsHue.node.ts` file now reflecting the ones listed in the Lights API documentation from https://developers.meethue.com/develop/hue-api/lights-api/. 

This has been tested against my personal Philips Hue setup, all operations worked fine.

title: Adding an optional database field to redis
body:
Hello, this is a tiny patch to add an optional field `database` to Redis credentials. It will allow users to select a different database for their operations instead of the default 0.

It defaults on `0` as in not to break any current implementations. The `select` operation will be run regardless the field is set or not, so maybe I can add a condition `if (database !== 0)` if necessary, but I think it is better to be explicit.

Looking forward to some feedback.
author: hnb2

title: ✨ Add chat messages to MS Teams node
body: This PR adds the ability to get and create messages in oneOnOne, meeting, and other Teams chats. * Add load options for available chats * Uses API version 1.0, which may require one of `Chat.Read` or `Chat.ReadWrite` permissions. Side note: I've noticed `beta` endpoints sometimes ignore required permissions on delegated access, e.g. the same endpoint for retrieving channel messages works on the beta version, but requires `ChannelMessage.Read.All` on `v1.0`. Updating the `channelMessage` endpoint will likely be a breaking change if users haven't added the correct permissions.

title: Add MongoDb Trigger node that works using change streams
body: A trigger node for MongoDb that outputs the change event from a change stream as a JSON. There is an option to output either the full document or only fields which have been changed

title: User management password reset flow
body:
**Mailing Setup**

1. Create a [Gmail app password](https://docs.n8n.io/credentials/imap/#using-gmail)
2. Set env vars

```
export N8N_UM_EMAIL_SMTP_USER=you@gmail.com
export N8N_UM_EMAIL_SMTP_PASS=yourAppPassword
export N8N_UM_EMAIL_MODE=smtp
export N8N_UM_EMAIL_SMTP_SENDER='"Your Name" <you@gmail.com>'
```

3. In /cli, `npm run build` to copy the HTML templates to /dist

author: krynble

title: Tomba node
body:
## Preparation - [x] Set up your editor for code formatting (indentation, new lines, linting). If you use Visual Studio Code, you can use the TSLint extension for linting.
- [x] Get credentials (e.g., Client ID, Client Secret, API key, user login, user password, website URL) for the service you are building a node for. ## Development - [x] Open a pull request as early as possible with `WIP` in the pull request title. - [x] If you are creating a node requested by a community member, make sure to comment on the feature request in the [community forum](https://community.n8n.io/c/feature-requests/5). - [x] Ensure complementary operations to each resource (e.g., create, delete) have been added. - [x] Ensure the node works with multiple items via one input. - [x] Ensure the parameters have the correct type. - [x] Mind the defaults: if the service has a default as true, keep it as true. Changing default values can break the existing workflows of the users. - [x] Check if the node disposes of everything properly, in particular, if connections were properly closed. - [x] Check your code using Nodelinter to ensure a clean lint before submitting your pull request ## Testing - [x] Test "create" and "update" operations with all fields/operations. - [x] Test the `continueOnFail` option with a Function node. (For example, a Widget node has a GET operation that takes a widgetId and returns information on the widget. To test that the workflow continues on fail, set the Widget node to continue on fail, create a Function node, return a valid and an invalid widgetId, connect the Function node to Widget node, and run the workflow. The Widget node should show two items: one with information on the widget and another one with the error from having passed an invalid ID.) ## Code formatting - [x] Ensure the branch lints cleanly by running `npm run lint`. - [x] Ensure the indentation is correct. Check this in the editorconfig. - [x] Ensure there are no extra spaces. Check this in the editorconfig. - [x] Code comment dividers inside if-branches. - [x] Use "create/delete" verbs for operations, except for tags, where you should use "add/remove". ## Errors and Outputs - [x] Ensure empty API responses return `{ success: true }`. - [x] Ensure the error responses are handled and displayed correctly (e.g., malformed requests, requests with invalid credentials) and use the current format. You can check this by making failing requests to the API. - [x] Check if the response can be simplified and add a simplify function (e.g., SecurityScorecard node). - [x] Ensure the response from `Create` is consistent with `Get`. - [x] Ensure the response from `Get All` is consistent with `Get`. ## Presentation - [x] Ensure the primary menu contains only required parameters. - [x] Ensure a JSON object is not shown in a single column in Table view. - [x] Make sure all GetAll operations have the fields `return` and `limit`. - [x] Ensure the property subtitle is set. - [x] Make sure the pagination (if any) is working correctly. Set Limit 1. ## Writing - [x] Ensure all descriptions are correct and end with a period. - [x] Ensure that most descriptions exist, excluding redundant ones. - [x] Ensure IDs in displayNames are capitalized (i.e.: "IDs", not "ids" or "Ids"). - [x] Ensure that IDs, if multiple, have descriptive qualifiers. - [x] Ensure the `name` property in `description` in the node class is written in camelCase. - [x] Ensure the file name and the Class name are identical. ## Branding - [x] Ensure the name of the service is written correctly (e.g., "GitHub" not "Github"). If the node is a trigger node, ensure it is named as such, by adding "Trigger" after the service name (e.g., "Trello Trigger"). - [x] Ensure the logo is either a PNG or SVG, ideally the latter. Vecta is a good website to find SVGs of different applications. - [x] If the logo is an SVG, ensure the canvas is a perfect square. If the logo is PNG, ensure it is 60x60 pixels and compressed. - [x] Ensure the border color of the node matches the branding of the service. ## Nice-to-haves (optional) - [x] Add handler for `continueOnFail`. This feature is included in some of the newest nodes (e.g Lemlist node) to continue the workflow even if the node's execution fails. - [x] Remove `required: false` and `description: ''` in the node descriptions (e.g., Lemlist node). - [x] At call site, specify first `body` and then `qs`. - [x] At call site, prepend the endpoint with slash `/` (e.g., "/campaign").

author: d3rwan

title: 🐛 Fixes icalendar node month out by one
body:
This fixes the month being out by one issue in the icalendar node.

**Linked issues:**
https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/issues/2627
https://community.n8n.io/t/icalendar-event-date-is-for-1-month-before-the-actual-event-date/7298
https://community.n8n.io/t/month-shifting-with-icalendar-node/6587

This will break any workflows where a user has already worked around the issue by using the Date & Time node to increment the month by one.
author: Joffcom
